Below there are two statements I and II. These statements may be either independent causes or may be effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of these statements may be the effect of the other statements. Read both the statements and decide which of the following answer choice correctly depicts the relationship between these two statements. Mark answer as
(A) If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
(B) If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
(C) If both the statements I and II are independent causes.
(D) If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes.
(E) If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
Statements:
I. Many people visited the religious place during the week-end.
II. Few people visited the religious place during the week days.

Below there are two statements I and II. These statements may be either independent causes or may be effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of these statements may be the effect of the other statements. Read both the statements and decide which of the following answer choice correctly depicts the relationship between these two statements. Mark answer as
(A) If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
(B) If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
(C) If both the statements I and II are independent causes.
(D) If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes.
(E) If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
Statements:
I. Police had resorted to lathi-charge to disperse the unruly mob from the civic headquarters.
II. The civic administration has recently hiked the property tax of the residential buildings by about 30 percent.

Below there are two statements I and II. These statements may be either independent causes or may be effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of these statements may be the effect of the other statements. Read both the statements and decide which of the following answer choice correctly depicts the relationship between these two statements. Mark answer as
(A) If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
(B) If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
(C) If both the statements I and II are independent causes.
(D) If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes.
(E) If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
Statements:
I. Large number of people living in the low lying areas has been evacuated during the last few days to safer places.
II. The Government has rushed in relief supplies to the people living in the affected areas.

Below there are two statements I and II. These statements may be either independent causes or may be effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of these statements may be the effect of the other statements. Read both the statements and decide which of the following answer choice correctly depicts the relationship between these two statements. Mark answer as
(A) If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
(B) If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
(C) If both the statements I and II are independent causes.
(D) If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes.
(E) If both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
Statements:
I. The Reserve Bank of India has recently put restrictions on few small banks in the country.
II. The small banks in the private and co-operative sector in India are not in a position to withstand the competitions of the bigger banks in the public sector.

Read the following information and answer the questions that follow:
1) A is older than B.
2) E & F are of equal age.
3) C is the youngest.
4) D is younger than F.
5) D is older than A.
Q-1) Which two of the above statements indicate that D is older than B?
A) 1 & 4         B) 4 & 5      C) 1 & 5      D) None of these.
Q-2) Which statement indicate that F is older than C?
A) 2 & 3          B) 3 only         C) 3 & 4       D) None of these.
Q-3) Which statements is/are not required to prove that B is younger than E?
A) 3 only      B) 3 & 4 only       C) 4 & 5        D) 3 & 5
Q-4) Which of the statements proves that A is younger than E?
A) 1, 2 & 4      B) 2, 4, & 5      C) 1, 4, & 5       D) None of these.

Read the following information and answer the questions that follow:
1) There is a group of six persons living in a four storeyed building.The persons are K, L, M, N, O & P. Each storey of the building has two flats: thus there are eight flats in all, two of them being empty.
2) The tallest of the group lives on the top floor while the shortest lives on the ground floor.
3) M is taller than L,who in turn is taller than P.
4) O is shorter than N.
5) One of the flat on first floor is vacant and one of the flat on second floor is vacant.
6) K is neither the tallest nor the shortest.
7) There are only two people taller than M, K is not one of them.
8) L shares the floor with the tallest among the group,while M shares it with the shortest.
Q-1) Who among the following is the tallest among the group?
A) N         B) M         C) K        D) Cannot be determined.
Q-2) Who does M share the floor with?
A) O        B) P        C) K        D) Cannot be determined.
Q-3) On which does K live?
A) Ground floor
B) First floor
C) Top floor
D) Cannot be determined.
Q-4) O & K live:
A) On the same floor
B) On two continuous floors.
C) With one floor between their floors.
D) Cannot be determined.
Q-5) Starting from the tallest of the group, what is the rank of K in order of height?
A) Fourth
B) Fifth
C) Sixth
D) Cannot be determined.
